title: chenopodium ayare
intro: Chenopodium ayare is a plant species native to the Andean region, scientifically classified as Chenopodium quinoa's closest wild relative. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health, as well as exhibiting potential anticancer properties. Chenopodium ayare can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, with some traditional herbal preparations also involving drying and grinding the leaves to make a powder for various medicinal uses.
